question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between the frequency and pitch of a sound wave?

options.label: A
options.text: Higher frequency sound waves produce a lower pitch

options.label: B
options.text: Higher frequency sound waves produce a higher pitch

options.label: C
options.text: Frequency and pitch are independent properties with no relationship

options.label: D
options.text: Pitch depends only on the amplitude of the sound wave

correct_answer: Higher frequency sound waves produce a higher pitch
explanation: Pitch is how high or low a sound seems to us. It depends directly on the frequency of the sound wave. Higher frequency means more vibrations per second, which our ears perceive as a higher pitch (like a whistle). Lower frequency means fewer vibrations per second, which sounds like a lower pitch (like a drum).
